usage: set_message [-help] [-id <list_of_msgIDs>] [-severity {warn error info reset} | -limit <number> | -no_limit  | -suppress  | -unsuppress  | -on_error {msg_only exit stop_script} | -stop_script_limit <number>]

options.name: -id
options.description: Specifies the list of msgIDs to be processed.

options.name: -limit
options.description: Sets the message limit for the selected messages. The limit must be non-negative.Limit zero will suppresses the message

options.name: -no_limit
options.description: Removes any existing limit on the selected messages. (Suppressed messages will not be un-suppressed.)

options.name: -on_error
options.description: Take the extra action for error message in -id <id_list>. Without -id option, the extra action applies to all error messages. Exit = exit immediately; stop_script = wait for command to finish and return to the Tcl prompt; msg_only = just print error message.

options.name: -severity
options.description: Specifies the new sverity for the messages being processed.

options.name: -stop_script_limit
options.description: Sets the message limit per every public command. If you set -stop_script_limit 10, at the 10th time the message being triggered for one command, the tool will wait for current Tcl command to finish, then stop executing any Tcl script, and return to the interactive Tcl prompt. The default value is -1, which is unlimited.
